title: A New Direction for Colombia and Implications for U.S. Policy
gist: Podcast episode notes (members-only teaser) for The Warcast: right-wing candidate Abelardo de la Espriella won Colombia's June 21 presidential election, and the episode discusses what his victory means for Colombia and U.S. regional policy, plus an update on Venezuela.

insights.claim: Right-wing candidate Abelardo de la Espriella won the Colombian presidency in a June 21 election.
insights.speaker: War on the Rocks
insights.so_what: A right-wing turn in Colombia could shift U.S.-Latin America alignment and regional policy on Venezuela.
insights.quote: right-wing candidate Abelardo de la Espriella won the presidency.

insights.claim: The episode frames de la Espriella's victory as having implications for U.S. policy in the region, with an accompanying update on Venezuela.
insights.speaker: War on the Rocks
insights.so_what: Signals Colombia-Venezuela dynamics as a watch item for U.S. regional and energy policy.
